page: Microsoft Azure M32-16ms is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the MS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8280M processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a base frequency of 2.7 GHz and includes 38 MB of L3 cache. The server is equipped with 875.0 GB of RAM, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 54.69 GB per core. It also includes 1049 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not feature any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear trade-off between single-core processing and multi-threaded or memory-intensive operations. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 1080.0 and a stress-ng single-core CPU rating in the bottom 25%. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 6670.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 11843.87. Memory bandwidth tests show average read-only speeds of 610.46 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 74.4 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Database operations, such as Redis SET, perform at an average level of 10.43 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the M32-16ms represents a high resource density configuration where the premium is placed on massive memory capacity rather than raw CPU compute power. This makes it a highly specialized option for workloads that require large in-memory datasets but do not demand top-tier single-threaded CPU performance. The server is best suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise applications, caching layers, and data warehousing workloads where the 875.0 GB RAM footprint prevents disk-paging bottlenecks.
description: Microsoft Azure M32-16ms is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MS family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8280M processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores. It features an exceptionally large memory capacity of 875.0 GB, translating to 54.69 GB of RAM per core, alongside 1049 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server is designed for memory-intensive workloads. Benchmark data reveals average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance, contrasted by weaker single-core processing capabilities. With a Redis SET throughput of 10.43 million operations per second and average database performance, this server is qualitatively optimized for high-capacity memory density rather than raw compute speed. It is highly suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring massive RAM allocations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M32-16ms is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 vCPUs, 875 GB of RAM, and 1049 GB of local storage, designed for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.

tagline: A memory-intensive virtual server featuring massive RAM capacity and local storage for demanding database and caching workloads.
